Short drive back to Magnolia with groceries in hand.

beats.activity: Relax at home: watch TV, scroll social media, wind down for the week.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down routine; Shaan relaxes before bed, reviewing the work week ahead and enjoying a quiet Sunday night.
Full 8 hours of sleep to prepare for Monday; the sleep beat spans overnight as required.

diary: Woke up early with the World Cup on my mind. Grabbed coffee at Starbucks in Magnolia and settled in to watch USA take on Wales—the energy was electric, even just watching on a café screen with other fans. Afterwards, met up with friends at Matt's in Pike Place Market for lunch. The sunshine was perfect, so we wandered through the market for a couple hours, just soaking it in and checking out the usual shops. Drove home around 1:30 since the Light Rail was still down. Hit up Whole Foods for a proper grocery haul—decided this week I'd actually meal prep instead of just ordering takeout like usual. Spent the evening chopping vegetables, cooking, and then just crashed on the couch with some TV. Felt good to have a slower day after the weekend rush.
thought_process: I definitely prioritized the World Cup match—it's rare I take a Monday morning off work vibes, but the timing felt right and I wasn't going to miss USA vs Wales. The Light Rail closure actually worked in my favor since I was already planning to drive anyway. Pike Place was a natural extension after lunch; the weather was too nice to rush home. By afternoon, I was in full adulting mode with the grocery run and meal prep, which honestly felt productive rather than like a chore. I think the sunny weather and the excitement of the match put me in a genuinely good headspace, so even mundane stuff like chopping onions felt manageable. By evening I was just tired and content.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
